results.resolved_title: Proteomic Analysis of the Type II Secretome Reveals New Proteins, Including Three Related Serine Proteases
results.quote: the T2S machinery transports cholera toxin, which induces profuse watery diarrhea, a hallmark of this life-threatening disease
results.note: ctxA is the A subunit of cholera toxin; abstract names cholera toxin as T2S-transported in V. cholerae N16961 El Tor.

results.resolved_title: Proteomic Analysis of the Type II Secretome Reveals New Proteins, Including Three Related Serine Proteases
results.quote: the T2S machinery transports cholera toxin, which induces profuse watery diarrhea, a hallmark of this life-threatening disease
results.note: ctxB is the B subunit of cholera toxin; abstract names cholera toxin as T2S-transported.

results.resolved_title: Proteomic Analysis of the Type II Secretome Reveals New Proteins, Including Three Related Serine Proteases
results.quote: four other proteins have been shown to be transported by the T2S machinery, including hemagglutinin protease, chitinase, GbpA, and lipase
results.note: hap = hemagglutinin protease (HA/protease), named as T2S-transported.

results.resolved_title: Proteomic Analysis of the Type II Secretome Reveals New Proteins, Including Three Related Serine Proteases
results.quote: This analysis identified 16 new putative T2S substrates, including sialidase, several proteins participating in chitin utilization, two aminopeptidases
results.note: nanH = sialidase/neuraminidase (VC1784); WebFetch of PMC3089498 Table1 confirms VC1784 sialidase as T2S substrate.

results.resolved_title: Proteomic Analysis of the Type II Secretome Reveals New Proteins, Including Three Related Serine Proteases
results.quote: four other proteins have been shown to be transported by the T2S machinery, including hemagglutinin protease, chitinase, GbpA, and lipase
results.note: GbpA named explicitly as T2S-transported (VCA0811).

results.resolved_title: Proteomic Analysis of the Type II Secretome Reveals New Proteins, Including Three Related Serine Proteases
results.quote: 16 new putative T2S substrates, including sialidase, several proteins participating in chitin utilization, two aminopeptidases, TagA-related protein, cytolysin
results.note: hlyA = V. cholerae cytolysin (VCA0219); named cytolysin among new T2S substrates.

results.note: Abstract names 16 new substrates generically; prtV (VCA0223) not named in retrieved abstract and full-text body was not returned by the PMC tool. Cannot verbatim-confirm prtV; abstract absence is not refutation.

results.resolved_title: Proteomic Analysis of the Type II Secretome Reveals New Proteins, Including Three Related Serine Proteases
results.quote: three serine proteases VesA, VesB, and VesC. Focusing on the initial characterization of VesA, VesB, and VesC, we have confirmed enzymatic activities and T2S-dependent transport for each of these proteases
results.note: VesA (VCA0803) named with confirmed T2S-dependent transport.

results.note: Effector VC_A0583 (Q9KM06); named as one of three hypothetical-protein T2S substrates.

results.resolved_title: Legionella pneumophila Secretes an Endoglucanase That Belongs to the Family-5 of Glycosyl Hydrolases and Is Dependent upon Type II Secretion
results.quote: Inactivation of, encoding a member of the family-5 of glycosyl hydrolases, abolished the endoglucanase activity in culture supernatants... Thus, CelA is the major secreted endoglucanase
results.note: CelA (lpg1918) is a T2S-dependent secreted endoglucanase of L. pneumophila; full text confirms T2S dependence.

results.resolved_title: Vitamin D3 Induces Autophagy in Human Monocytes/Macrophages via Cathelicidin
results.note: DOI resolves (CrossRef + Europe PMC) to Yuk et al. 2009 Cell Host Microbe 6(3):231-243 on vitamin D3/cathelicidin/autophagy in human macrophages (Mycobacterium). No IcsB, no Shigella, no T3SS. Provenance defect: cited DOI is the wrong paper entirely.

results.resolved_title: Causalities of war: The connection between type VI secretion system and microbiota
results.note: Resolved paper is a T6SS/microbiota review (Allsopp, Bernal, Nolan, Filloux). Full text retrieved: no mention of CteG, Chlamydia trachomatis, or a T3SS effector CteG. Wrong paper for a Chlamydia T3SS effector (also wrong system: T6SS review vs T3SS).

results.resolved_title: Cloning and characterization of the Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae-RTX-toxin III (ApxIII) gene.
results.quote: we found that ApxIII could be secreted from E. coli by the heterologous RTX transporter proteins HlyB and HlyD... the apxIIIA gene coded for the structural RTX toxin
results.note: ApxIIIA is the structural RTX toxin of A. pleuropneumoniae secreted by the HlyB/HlyD (T1SS) ABC transporter; consistent with T1SS effector. Abstract retrieved (full-text body empty).

results.resolved_title: Type II Secretion by Aeromonas salmonicida: Evidence for Two Periplasmic Pools of Proaerolysin
results.note: Paper (abstract only; full-text body empty) is about T2S/type II secretion of proaerolysin in Aeromonas. Abstract never names a gene 'ash3'; aerolysin gene is typically aerA. Gene 'ash3' not in retrieved abstract, body not retrievable, so cannot confirm or positively refute the mapping. INACCESSIBLE.

results.resolved_title: Dietary trehalose enhances virulence of epidemic Clostridium difficile
results.note: Full text retrieved: paper is about dietary trehalose and epidemic Clostridium difficile ribotypes RT027/RT078 (treR, ptsT). No Legionella, no MavC, no T4SS effector. Wrong paper for L. pneumophila MavC.

results.resolved_title: Molecular mechanism of elongation factor 1A inhibition by a Legionella pneumophila glycosyltransferase
results.quote: we showed that two further glycosyltransferases possessed the conserved UDP-glucose-binding sites and EF1A-binding grooves, and are, like LpGT, translocated into the macrophage through the Icm/Dot (intracellular multiplication/defect in organelle trafficking) system
results.note: LpGT = Lgt1/lpg1368; full text confirms translocation into macrophage via the Icm/Dot type IV secretion system (T4SS effector) of L. pneumophila.

results.resolved_title: A Burkholderia pseudomallei Type III Secreted Protein, BopE, Facilitates Bacterial Invasion of Epithelial Cells and Exhibits Guanine Nucleotide Exchange Factor Activity
results.quote: We report the characterization of BopE, a type III secreted protein that is encoded adjacent to the locus and is homologous to SopE/SopE2
results.note: BopE is named a type III secreted protein. Title/abstract describe Burkholderia pseudomallei; batch lists B. thailandensis E264 (close relative, BTH_II0874). Effector/T3SS role and protein identity confirmed; organism is the closely related Burkholderia species in the original paper.

results.resolved_title: A family of Type VI secretion system effector proteins that form ion-selective pores
results.quote: Ssp6 (SMDB11_4673) was identified as a small effector secreted by the T6SS of Serratia marcescens Db10 in previous studies using a mass spectrometry approach
results.note: Ssp6 (locus SMDB11_4673) confirmed as a T6SS-secreted antibacterial pore-forming effector of Serratia marcescens; matches batch locus_tag.

results.resolved_title: NadA, a Novel Vaccine Candidate of Neisseria meningitidis
results.quote: NadA has a predicted molecular structure strikingly similar to a novel class of adhesins (YadA and UspA2), forms high molecular weight oligomers, and binds to epithelial cells in vitro
results.note: NadA is an oligomeric surface-exposed outer-membrane adhesin of N. meningitidis MC58 with YadA/UspA2-type architecture, consistent with a type V secretion (trimeric autotransporter) protein. Full text confirms surface exposure, OM anchoring, host-cell binding.
